title: Race-lap quota model plus within-group renormalization
summary: A next-lap pit event is partly a competition within each Year-Race-Lap group: only a small subset of drivers pit on the following lap.
rationale: A next-lap pit event is partly a competition within each Year-Race-Lap group: only a small subset of drivers pit on the following lap. Public F1 strategy material frames pit windows and strategy as field-level phenomena shaped by traffic and rival actions. A separate group-level estimate of how many cars are likely to pit next lap can regularize overconfident independent binary scores.
implementation_hint: Train a secondary model to predict the expected count or rate of next-lap pitters for each Year-Race-Lap regime using leakage-safe aggregates only. At inference, rescale driver-level probabilities within each race-lap via temperature/softmax or rank-preserving normalization so their group sum matches the expected count. Blend this postprocessed score with the raw binary model instead of replacing it.
expected_effect: Small-to-moderate leaderboard gain through better ordering near pit windows and reduced false positives on laps where almost nobody should stop.
risk: If normalization is too aggressive it can hurt global AUC, since AUC is not a per-group metric. The group prior must be estimated out-of-fold; using actual group target counts from train folds would leak badly.
